Methods

Gene prediction was performed using the BRAKER4 pipeline (Krall & Hoff, 2026) implemented in Snakemake (Mölder et al., 2021), running in EP/BRAKER2 mode (Bruna et al., 2021). Protein evidence was generated using ProtHint (Bruna et al., 2020), which produced 582,327 protein-based hints by aligning the protein database to the genome using DIAMOND (Buchfink et al., 2021) and Spaln (Iwata & Gotoh, 2012). ProtHint was re-run in a second iteration using the initial AUGUSTUS predictions as additional seeds, producing refined protein hints. GeneMark-EP+ (Bruna et al., 2020) was trained with protein-derived intron hints and predicted 33,604 genes. GeneMark predictions were filtered for genes with evidence support, yielding 18,605 high-quality training gene candidates. After etraining filtering, 8,000 of 18,605 genes were retained. Redundant sequences were then removed using DIAMOND (Buchfink et al., 2021), reducing the training set from 8,000 to 7,678 loci. Training genes were then downsampled using a Poisson distribution (downsample_traingenes.pl, lambda=2) to reduce single-exon gene over-representation, yielding 6,464 of 7,678 genes. The training set was split into 6,164 genes for training and 300 genes held out for accuracy assessment. AUGUSTUS (Stanke et al., 2008) species-specific parameters were trained on the selected gene set using etraining and further refined using optimize_augustus.pl. AUGUSTUS predicted 34,773 genes using the trained parameters and all available extrinsic evidence as hints. Gene predictions from AUGUSTUS and GeneMark were merged using TSEBRA (Gabriel et al., 2021). The final gene set contains 35,109 genes and 38,114 transcripts after filtering for internal stop codons and CDS normalization. The final annotation was converted to GFF3 format using AGAT (Dainat, 2024). Quality was assessed using BUSCO (Manni et al., 2021), compleasm (Huang & Li, 2023) with miniprot (Li, 2023), gffcompare (Pertea & Pertea, 2020).

References

If you publish these BRAKER4 results, please cite the following publications:

  1. Krall, H., & Hoff, K. J. (2026). BRAKER4: a Snakemake pipeline for fully automated eukaryotic genome annotation. In preparation.
  2. Molder, F., et al. (2021). Sustainable data analysis with Snakemake. F1000Research, 10, 33. doi:10.12688/f1000research.29032.2
  3. Huang, N., & Li, H. (2023). compleasm: a faster and more accurate reimplementation of BUSCO. Bioinformatics, 39(10), btad595. doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/btad595
  4. Li, H. (2023). Protein-to-genome alignment with miniprot. Bioinformatics, 39(1), btad014. doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/btad014
  5. Lomsadze, A., Ter-Hovhannisyan, V., Chernoff, Y. O., & Borodovsky, M. (2005). Gene identification in novel eukaryotic genomes by self-training algorithm. Nucleic Acids Research, 33(20), 6494-6506. doi:10.1093/nar/gki937
  6. Bruna, T., Lomsadze, A., & Borodovsky, M. (2020). GeneMark-EP+: eukaryotic gene prediction with self-training in the space of genes and proteins. NAR Genomics and Bioinformatics, 2(2), lqaa026. doi:10.1093/nargab/lqaa026
  7. Buchfink, B., Reuter, K., & Drost, H.-G. (2021). Sensitive protein alignments at tree-of-life scale using DIAMOND. Nature Methods, 18, 366-368. doi:10.1038/s41592-021-01101-x
  8. Iwata, H., & Gotoh, O. (2012). Benchmarking spliced alignment programs including Spaln2, an extended version of Spaln that incorporates additional species-specific features. Nucleic Acids Research, 40(20), e161. doi:10.1093/nar/gks708
  9. Bruna, T., Hoff, K. J., Lomsadze, A., Stanke, M., & Borodovsky, M. (2021). BRAKER2: Automatic Eukaryotic Genome Annotation with GeneMark-EP+ and AUGUSTUS Supported by a Protein Database. NAR Genomics and Bioinformatics, 3(1), lqaa108. doi:10.1093/nargab/lqaa108
  10. Stanke, M., Diekhans, M., Baertsch, R., & Haussler, D. (2008). Using native and syntenically mapped cDNA alignments to improve de novo gene finding. Bioinformatics, 24(5), 637-644. doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/btn013

  12. Bruna, T., Gabriel, L., & Hoff, K. J. (2025). Navigating Eukaryotic Genome Annotation Pipelines: A Route Map to Using BRAKER, Galba, and TSEBRA. Methods in Molecular Biology, 2935, 67-107. doi:10.1007/978-1-0716-4583-3_4
  13. Pertea, G., & Pertea, M. (2020). GFF Utilities: GffRead and GffCompare. F1000Research, 9, 304. doi:10.12688/f1000research.23297.2
